div.p: William by the grace of God King of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting: Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, the donation which William de Vieuxpont made to God and to Holy Rood at Edinburgh and to the Canons there serving God, of the land of Ogilface, by its correct bounds and with all its just pertinents. For them to hold in free and pure and perpetual alms, with all things justly pertaining to the aforesaid land, so freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforesaid William de Vieuxpont made for them thereupon bears witness. Reserving my service from that land both in aids and in other things from the heirs of the aforementioned William. Witness, Walter my chaplain, Philip de Valognes my chamberlain, William Comyn, William del Bois, David de la Haye, William of Moray, Richard Revel, Henry Revel. At Forfar on the fourth day of November. Given by the hand of William del Bois, in the time of Florence the chancellor, in the year of the Incarnation of the Lord, 1203.
